text: We present the Stanford CoreNLP toolkit, an integrated framework designed to provide a comprehensive suite of linguistic analysis tools for English. The pipeline offers a robust set of natural language processing annotations, including tokenization, sentence splitting, part-of-speech tagging, morphological analysis, named entity recognition, syntactic parsing (both constituency and dependency-based), and coreference resolution. Developed to be both flexible and efficient, CoreNLP allows users to easily apply a wide array of text analysis technology to large corpora. The system is designed with a focus on architectural consistency, providing a simplified API that enables researchers and practitioners to extract high-quality linguistic features with minimal configuration. Since its release, the toolkit has become a foundational resource in both academia and industry, supported by a modular design that facilitates the integration of new components and languages.
